SnowDistribution: Winds approx 10 - 20 mph fresh, cooling. Good visibility.  Scoured steep areas requiring crampons with serious run out potential.
Comment: Freezing level will rise above summits during Thursday with some rain. Into Friday and the weekend, frontal systems bring rain, strong to gale force winds, and freezing levels hovering around or just above the summits.
WeatherInfluences: There will be moderate precipitation throughout the period, initially snow above 750 metres, but turning to rain at all levels by the afternoon. Winds will be strong to very strong, South South-Westerly. During the morning the freezing level will rise sharply from 800 metres to above the summits and remain there.
SnowStability: Above 800m, on West, through North and East to South aspects, existing isolated windslab, on steep sheltered locations, will undergo a period of instability through the day as the temperatures rise and rain falls to the summits. Although not extensive in either area or depth, this windslab will be prone to wet instabilities, and on very steep locations small avalanches are possible. Corrie flanks, cross loaded gullies and gully tops will be most affected. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: After a dry morning, some very light precipitation developed from the west, falling as snow above 600 metres. Winds were fresh to strong, mainly Southerly, with the freezing level around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Isolated unstable windslab continues to persist in steep, wind sheltered locations above 800 metres, on mainly North-East through East, to Southerly aspects. Fresh recent unstable windslab, is also found on West to North aspects, again above 800 metres in steep wind sheltered locations. Scoured snow remains firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Wind scoured and firm on many aspects. Mostly clear in the morning with reduced visibility in the afternoon. Winds 40 - 50 mph, progress difficult.
Comment: Milder on Friday and milder still on Saturday.
WeatherInfluences: Strong South-South-East winds will become very strong during the day and veer to the South-West. Snow showers, heaviest in the morning, turning to rain below 1100 metres in the afternoon when the freezing level rises to around 1250 metres.
SnowStability: Fresh unstable windslab will develop mainly on West through North to East aspects, initially above 800 metres and only above 1200 metres by the afternoon. Wet snow instabilities will develop during the day on the same aspects mainly between 800 and 1100 metres. Other aspects will be scoured with good stability. Fresh fragile cornices at higher elevations and wet thawing cornices lower down. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry morning with a little very light snow in the afternoon. Gale force South-South-Easterly winds. The temperature was slowly rising with the freezing level around 600 metres at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: Localised areas of moderately bonded windslab are present in wind sheltered locations mainly on West through North to North-East aspects above 800 metres. Slopes that have been exposed to the wind are scoured firm and stable. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Areas of wind scoured firm snow and bare ground, some terrain is hard going on foot due to firm windslab collapsing onto soft snow beneath.  Cloud capping the tops with some bright spells. Winds approx 40 - 50 mph making progress very difficult and strenuous.
Comment: All change during Friday as the freezing level rises towards the summits during the day.
WeatherInfluences: Light snowfall is expected during the night on strong Southerly winds.  During the day winds will increase to gale force and veer South-Westerly. By late morning light snow will turn to rain just below summit levels. The 800 metre freezing level will rise to near summit levels by midday.
SnowStability: Initially new unstable windslab will develop on North-West to North-East aspects above 600 metres. As it turns milder  on these aspects above 800 metres, avalanches and cornice collapse are likely. Areas especially affected will be steep locations such as gullies, coire back walls, crag aprons and convex terrain. Below 800 metres and above 500 metres, instabilities will persist on steep, convex slopes and burn lines. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A mostly dry day with gale force South-South-Easterly winds. The freezing level at midday was just above 600 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Drifting snow continued at higher levels. Poorly bonded windslab is present on steep North-West to North-East aspects. This is most widespread on steep wind sheltered slopes above 800 metres. West and East facing slopes are affected by cross loading. Below 800 metres and above 500 metres, unstable windslab is present on steep banks, and burn lines. Fragile cornices overhang some of these aspects. Elsewhere the snowpack is becoming wind eroded and firm. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow - ice on most aspects.  Cloud base was at 700 metres all day. Winds 30-40 mph, progress difficult and tiring.
Comment: Freezing level rising during the day, around the summits by afternoon with some rain.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level will be around 600 metres overnight but will start to rise, forecasted to be around 1200 metres by early afternoon. Precipitation will initially fall as snow, turning to rain as the freezing level rises. Winds, initially South-South-East, veering to the West by the end of the day. Gale force during the day.
SnowStability: New unstable windslab will develop, mainly on South-West through North to East aspects above 900 metres.  As freezing levels rise, deposits will become increasingly unstable with avalanches likely. Greatest hazard will be found on any steep terrain such as corrie rims, gully exits and scarp slopes. Cornices will be prone to collapse above these locations.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A cloudy but dry day. The freezing level was slowly rising, to around 600 metres by mid afternoon. Winds, initially South-East but veering to the South by the end of the day. Gale force throughout.
ObservedSnowStability: Moderately to weakly bonded windslab is found mainly on West to North aspects above 900 metres with newly formed cornices above these slopes. Greatest hazard is found in steep wind sheltered terrain such as corrie rims and gully exits. Localised areas of moderately bonded windslab is also found on South-West, North-East and East aspects and at lower elevations. Fragile cornices exist above these slopes. Wind exposed terrain is scoured and very icy.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Very hard snow ice on exposed windward slopes.  Good visibility.  Winds 20-30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts.
Comment: Period of poorer stability expected after midday.
WeatherInfluences: Distinct weather change in prospect. Snowfall throughout the period, heaviest during the morning, with rain at lower levels from dawn onwards. Freezing level at 700m overnight then rising to summits from morning onwards. Moderate Southerly or South-Westerly winds becoming strong during the morning.
SnowStability: Period of poor stability with the onset of milder temperatures after earlier snowfall and drifting. Most affected locations will be steep North-West through North to East aspects above 900m. Recently formed cornices will weaken later. Some minor wet snow instabilities possible on very steep ground as low as 800m during the afternoon.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Dry and cold with moderate South-Easterly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Highly variable picture of stability and instability on many lee slopes. In places, near surface instability in softer snow alongside well bonded or crusty older snow. Elsewhere, some mid pack and deeper instability though bridged over by dense, strong layers. Most affected areas are West through North to North-East aspects above 950m. Good stability at all altitudes on East to South-West aspects that have been recently wind scoured.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Snow cover above 400 metres with some deeper accumulations and drifted snow above 800 metres. Moderate South-Easterly winds 15-20 mph at summit level. Reasonable visibility at most levels.
Comment: There is some uncertainly with the rain and snow amounts in the forecast. Computer model show quite a bit of rain where as precipitation could fall as snow. Please check freezing levels to adapt to conditions. The general trend is for milder weather.
WeatherInfluences: Overnight, the freezing level will start to rise and will be above the summits by early morning then lowering to around 1100 metres in the afternoon. Some light snow showers at first on Thursday night mainly above 700 metres these showers becoming increasing moist, rain is also possibility then sleet and snow again mainly above 950 metres. Winds will strengthen and veer through South ending up Westerly, for the most part of the day strong South-Westerly will prevail.
SnowStability: New snow accumulations are not expected to be extensive. The snowpack will slowly become moist leading to wet snow instabilities on all steep slopes below 900 metres. Above 950 metres North-West through North to Easterly aspects will get the deepest new deposits. Sheltered gullies and steep coire rims will be particularly affected. Cornices will be unstable and prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Today the strong, cold South-Easterly winds of yesterday eased quite considerably to moderate and brief periods of sunshine broke through to give a bright day. The freezing level rose to around 700 metres by midday and it remained dry throughout.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of poorly bonded windslab still persist in many wind sheltered locations. Greatest accumulations are generally on terrain above 850 metres particularly in sheltered gullies and on steep slopes with a North-West to North-East aspect. Fragile cornices also exist above similar areas. Cross loading has affected some other wind sheltered aspects. Wind exposed slopes are firm and often icy with better stability. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
